scope: two synthesis Loop Lab candidates (track-synthesis/loop-01, loop-02) judged against their parents editorial-02 and generative-02
criteria_scale: 1-5, maximize; visual_hierarchy is the primary criterion
promotion: none — this file judges and does not promote a champion

evidence_inspected.not_read: No other synthesis judge files (gemini/terra synthesis) were read or referenced, per instruction.
independent_checks_performed: Re-parsed all four SVGs (loop-01/02 card + variants) with xml.etree.ElementTree: all well-formed, single <svg> root, viewBox '0 0 1200 630', role=img + aria-labelledby + title/desc, no <script>, no xlink:href, no external assets (only http:// occurrence is the SVG namespace).
Recomputed every WCAG 2.x contrast pair from raw hex rather than trusting the reported metrics.

Ran an independent uppercase-serif-bold glyph-width estimate (factors 0.58-0.72) to sanity-check the loop-01 overset, the loop-02 venue repair, and both title/panel safe-zones. I did NOT run Inkscape myself, so exact sub-pixel bboxes are trusted-not-verified; my estimates corroborate them directionally and do not flip any loop-02 gate.

independent_findings: Contrast reproduces exactly: CTA white on #173B57 = 11.67:1 (pass); labels #5E5D58 on paper = 5.87:1; teal kicker #006E70 on paper = 5.39:1; badges white-on-accent 6.06-7.02:1; module muted #BFD9DD on #171C32 = 11.36:1. Every value in both layout-metrics files matched my recomputation.
The generative-02 CTA defect I logged earlier reproduces (white on #3B82F6 = 3.68:1) and is genuinely repaired in both synthesis loops via #173B57 (11.67:1).

Loop-01 overset is REAL and VISIBLE: 'HUB A + WEB' in the hybrid compact card renders past the card edge (reported x=779.358 vs card x=776) and is clipped to 'HUB A + WEE' in variants-preview.png; my glyph estimate lands ~779-789px at realistic caps factors, confirming it. The track honestly self-reports no_clipping=fail / overall_pass=false.
Loop-01 display title safe-zone passes but is thin: 68.738px vs a 64px floor (~4.7px headroom); my 0.72 caps factor estimate grazes/exceeds the 696px limit, so the firewall is not robust.

Loop-02 repairs the overset by promoting VENUE to a full-width row: all three long venues clear their cards by 82-128px and the dense three-line title clears the panel by 111.516px. My glyph estimates corroborate comfortable margins at every factor tested.

Loop-02 proves the routing module is a generalizable brand system (Routing Module A vs B, distinct patterns, event-tied labels), directly curing the 'palette-not-a-system' weakness I logged against generative-02, and adds resilience rules (density tiers, wideVenue metadata mode, content-driven badge clamp) that also cure editorial-02's fixed-badge brittleness.

integration_assessment.question: Does the synthesis truly integrate both parents, or decorate one with the other?
integration_assessment.verdict: True structural integration in both loops. The editorial grid/hierarchy/token-system is load-bearing structure and the generative routing module is a load-bearing, full-height brand surface — neither is ornament on the other. tokens.json carries source_lineage from both parents and encodes both idioms as one system with an explicit signalModule->editorialZone firewall.
integration_assessment.beats_parents: synthesis-02 is Pareto-superior to both parents: it matches editorial-02's 5/5 hierarchy/clarity/coherence, adds the brand distinctiveness editorial-02 lacked (4->5), and dominates generative-02's system/clarity weaknesses (2/3->5/5) while keeping its 5 distinctiveness and repairing its CTA/title defects.

synthesis-01.rationale: The best-of-both-worlds combination image in the lineage: editorial-02's title-first grid on the left, generative-02's concentric-ring routing module on the right, with both flagged parent defects repaired (CTA 3.68->11.67:1, title/panel intrusion replaced by an 80px gutter). The display card is production-clean. But the shipped three-variant sheet visibly clips the hybrid compact venue ('HUB A + WEB' -> 'HUB A + WEE'), disproving its own published 11-char venue budget; the track honestly reports overall_pass=false. Clarity, coherence, and polish are pulled down by that containment failure and by a thin (4.7px) title firewall.
top_defects: Blocking: compact hybrid venue overset (x=779.358 vs card x=776), visible in variants-preview.png; disproves the token file's 11-char venue budget.
Thin title/panel safe-zone: 68.738px vs 64px floor (~4.7px headroom); grazes under a stricter glyph metric.
Fixed-width compact metadata columns and fixed badge widths (same brittleness class editorial-02 was flagged for).
synthesis-01.comparison_to_parents: Exceeds both parents on brand_distinctiveness (fuses two ownable idioms) and matches editorial-02 on display-card hierarchy, but regresses below editorial-02 on production readiness because it ships a visible overset that editorial-02 did not.

objective_gates.required_content: pass (present, but one variant venue is partially clipped)
layout_quality_defects: Compact hybrid venue 'HUB A + WEB' escapes card by 3.358px and is visibly clipped in variants-preview.png; track self-reports no_clipping=fail / overall_pass=false.

synthesis-02.rationale: The strongest true synthesis in the set and Pareto-superior to both parents. It keeps generative-02's ownable routing module (and proves it generalizes via a distinct Module B pattern and event-tied labels), adopts editorial-02's title-first hierarchy, and extends editorial-02's variant discipline into a genuine resilience system (density tiers, width-aware wideVenue metadata, content-driven badge clamp, full CTA state contracts, a deliberate stress fixture). It repairs its predecessor's overset with rendered evidence (long venues clear by 82-128px; dense title clears the panel by 111.516px) and keeps the CTA at 11.67:1. Held from a perfect polish score only by an honest system-font dependency (no embedding fallback), untested localization/RTL, and a slightly dense left column.
top_defects: Environment-dependent glyph widths: <text> relies on Georgia/Segoe UI fallbacks with no embedding strategy; margins hold in the Inkscape render but exact widths vary by environment.
Localization/RTL untested: resilience proven for English lengths only (the track names this as its own next hypothesis).
Left-column compositional density: full-width venue row + two rules make the dense card tighter than loop-01.
synthesis-02.comparison_to_parents: Equals or beats both parents on every criterion: matches editorial-02's 5/5 hierarchy/clarity/coherence and adds distinctiveness (4->5); dominates generative-02 by curing its hollow token system (2->5) and misreported/borderline contrast and title-intrusion defects while retaining its 5 distinctiveness.

objective_gates.overset_repair: pass (venue promoted to full-width row; stress-tested on longer strings, not by content substitution)
objective_gates.required_content: pass (card + 3 variants incl. an explicit long-content stress variant)

comparison.loop_over_loop: synthesis-02 strictly improves on synthesis-01: information_clarity 3->5, system_coherence 3->5, production_polish 2->4, with hierarchy and distinctiveness held at 5. The single decisive difference is layout_quality: loop-01 fails (visible compact-venue overset), loop-02 passes robustly after a real, measured, stress-tested repair.
comparison.method_quality: This is a healthy build->measure->repair loop: loop-01 honestly exposed its own rendered overset (overall_pass=false) instead of hiding it, and loop-02 repaired it at the system level (width-aware metadata rule) rather than by swapping in shorter content.

preference_reason: It is the only synthesis candidate that couples top hierarchy, brand distinctiveness, and information clarity with a resilience-grade token system and passes every objective gate under my own independent verification — and it is Pareto-superior to both parents rather than a decoration of one with the other.
dissent: I register explicit, preserved dissent rather than laundering a single 'best in everything' verdict. (1) I preserve the parent-panel split: Gemini preferred generative-02 for pure visual impact; the synthesis does NOT flatten this — the generative routing module remains the primary brand surface. On raw single-image drama alone, generative-02's full graphic hemisphere is still arguably more arresting than synthesis-02's cleaner-but-busier editorial left column, and I hold that aesthetic tension deliberately; on the whole brief (hierarchy + reuse + gates + honest measurement) synthesis-02 clearly wins. (2) I dissent from any reading that dismisses synthesis-01: its display card is production-clean and its self-reported failure is methodologically valuable; only its variant sheet fails. (3) Attribution caveat: the synthesis artifacts and their Inkscape metrics are generated/self-measured by gpt-5.6-sol. I independently reproduced all contrast (exact) and corroborated geometry direction via glyph estimates, but I did not run Inkscape, so exact sub-pixel bboxes are trusted-not-verified; none of my independent estimates flip a loop-02 gate, and my estimate independently confirms the loop-01 overset (also visible in the pixel preview). (4) I am judging only and promote no champion; I concur with the track's own needs_human_review flag to the extent that a human should confirm the two pixel previews, which I inspected and found clean for loop-02.
notes: svg_validity and layout_quality are objective pass/fail gates; the 1-5 criteria are subjective and maximized with visual_hierarchy primary. No other synthesis judge's files were read or modified. All contrast figures were recomputed independently; geometry claims were checked with an independent glyph estimate and cross-referenced against the rendered PNG previews.
